title: salix lasiolepis var. bigelovii
intro: Salix lasiolepis var. bigelovii, also known as Bigelow's willow, is a variety of willow tree native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a deciduous shrub or small tree with medicinal properties, particularly in its bark, which contains salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin. The medicinal properties of Bigelow's willow are used to reduce inflammation, relieve pain, and treat fever, making it a valuable herb for treating various health conditions, including arthritis and headaches. Bigelow's willow can be prepared into herbal teas, salves, and infusions, which are used to promote relaxation, improve sleep quality, and alleviate menstrual cramps.
